title: FileHeader.total_chunks has no upper bound; resource exhaustion via large value
description: messages.rs FileHeader accepts total_chunks: u32 with no upper limit. A malicious peer can send total_chunks: 4294967295, causing the receiver to wait for billions of FileChunk messages that never arrive. If the receiver pre-allocates based on total_chunks (e.g. a Vec with capacity), this also causes an OOM. No validation rejects an implausibly large chunk count relative to size_bytes. Fix: add a deserializer or post-deserialization check that rejects total_chunks > some reasonable ceiling (e.g. ceil(size_bytes / MIN_CHUNK_SIZE)) or a hard cap.
